examples.title: Basic Pipe Communication (Parent-Child)
examples.description: Demonstrates creating pipe, forking, and communicating between parent and child
examples.explanation: Creates pipe before fork. Child writes message, parent reads it. Unused ends must be closed for proper communication.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is message size

examples.title: Implementing Shell Pipeline (ls | wc)
examples.description: Implements shell command 'ls | wc -l' using pipe() and exec()
examples.explanation: Creates pipe, forks two children. First child redirects stdout to pipe (ls), second redirects stdin (wc -l). Parent waits for both.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is output size of ls

examples.title: Named Pipe (FIFO) Server-Client
examples.description: Demonstrates named pipe communication between unrelated processes
examples.explanation: mkfifo() creates named pipe. One process opens for writing (blocks until reader opens), other for reading. Communication like regular file I/O.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is data transferred

examples.title: Bidirectional Pipe Communication
examples.description: Shows how to create bidirectional communication using two pipes
examples.explanation: Creates two pipes in opposite directions. Parent->child uses pipe1, child->parent uses pipe2. Both processes close unused ends properly.
